name: Aztec Empire
declineFactors: Alliance of conquered peoples with Spanish
description:
The Aztec Empire (1428–1521 CE) dominated central Mexico from its island capital of Tenochtitlan, one of the largest cities in the world at the time with a population of over 200,000. The city was built on an island in Lake Texcoco, connected to the mainland by causeways.

The Aztecs developed sophisticated agricultural techniques (chinampas), a complex calendar system, monumental architecture, and an extensive tribute network. The empire fell to Hernán Cortés and his indigenous allies in 1521, accelerated by devastating smallpox epidemics.
